proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the binary features is associated with higher objective_response rate (univariate chi-square).
The ordinal feature_004 (values 0/1/2) shows a monotone decreasing relationship with objective_response.
Among the continuous features, several (e.g., feature_020, feature_024) are negatively associated with objective_response in univariate logistic regression.

analyses.code: for c in binary: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Three binary features have very strong positive univariate effects on objective_response: feature_014 (resp 23.5% vs 16.7%, diff +6.7pp, p=5.8e-70), feature_001 (22.1% vs 16.5%, +5.6pp, p=1.9e-55), feature_021 (22.0% vs 16.6%, +5.4pp, p=6.6e-51). Two binary features have small but significant negative effects: feature_026 (16.8% vs 19.1%, p=3.3e-6) and feature_009 (16.6% vs 19.0%, p=3.5e-5). The remaining 11 binary features (010, 005, 016, 025, 019, 015, 008, 018, 006, 032, 030) are not significant.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_004')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_004'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 shows a clear monotone negative gradient: response rates 23.1% (n=17592), 17.4% (n=24971), 12.9% (n=7437) for values 0/1/2. Chi-square p=1.8e-90.

analyses.code: univariate Logit for each continuous feature on objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Five continuous features are significant univariately: feature_020 (beta=-0.036, p=1.1e-30), feature_024 (beta=-0.005, p=2.4e-16), feature_029 (beta=+0.118, p=2.9e-7), feature_027 (beta=-0.006, p=6.1e-6), feature_022 (beta=-0.002, p=1.9e-4). The remaining continuous features (007, 031, 028, 013, 033, 034, 035, 023, 003, 002, 017, 011, 012) are not significant univariately.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model that adjusts for all 35 features simultaneously, the same set of features identified univariately remain significant predictors of objective_response, suggesting they capture independent information rather than confounding.

Adjusted effect sizes for feature_014, feature_001, and feature_021 will remain large and positive on the log-odds scale, with feature_004 and feature_020 carrying the largest negative adjusted effects.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X_all_features_standardized)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit with all 35 features (continuous standardized). Adjusted log-odds coefficients (z-stat) — Positive: feature_014 +0.436 (z=17.9), feature_001 +0.374 (z=16.0), feature_021 +0.362 (z=15.4), feature_029 +0.061 (z=5.3), feature_007 +0.026 (z=2.3). Negative: feature_004 -0.362 (z=-20.4), feature_020 -0.142 (z=-11.6), feature_024 -0.100 (z=-8.6), feature_026 -0.169 (z=-5.0), feature_009 -0.188 (z=-4.6), feature_027 -0.060 (z=-4.5), feature_022 -0.048 (z=-3.8). All other features non-significant. Pseudo-R^2=0.032; LLR p=1.4e-302.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three binary features with strongest positive main effects (feature_014, feature_001, feature_021) are correlated with each other (i.e., they tend to co-occur), explaining their similar effect sizes in the marginal univariate analyses.

When patients are stratified by all 8 combinations of (feature_001, feature_021, feature_014), the response rate is approximately equal in every cell (~17%) except in the cell where all three are simultaneously positive, indicating a multiplicative (3-way) interaction rather than three independent additive main effects.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_014'], df['feature_001']); pd.crosstab(df['feature_014'], df['feature_021']); pd.crosstab(df['feature_001'], df['feature_021'])
analyses.result_summary: The three binaries are essentially independent of one another (joint frequencies near the product of marginals). E.g., feature_014=1 has 40% feature_001=1 prevalence vs 40% in feature_014=0 group; same pattern for the other pairs. So co-occurrence does NOT explain the similar marginal effect sizes.

analyses.code: df.groupby(['feature_001','feature_021','feature_014'])['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count'])
analyses.result_summary: Eight-cell stratification: 7 of 8 cells have response 15.7-17.2% (n 3585-12533 each). The single cell (feature_001=1 ∧ feature_021=1 ∧ feature_014=1) has response 63.3% (n=2401). The pattern is overwhelmingly a 3-way interaction: no benefit from any one or two of the binaries, but a +46pp jump when all three are positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic regression model that includes the main effects, all 2-way products, and the 3-way product (feature_001 × feature_021 × feature_014) will show a large positive 3-way coefficient with the 2-way and main coefficients near zero, formally confirming the 3-way interaction.
A binary indicator triple_pos = (feature_001=1 ∧ feature_021=1 ∧ feature_014=1) will be a strong, independent predictor of objective_response after adjustment for all 35 baseline features.

analyses.code: Logit with main effects a,b,c; 2-way ab,ac,bc; and 3-way abc among feature_001, feature_021, feature_014.
analyses.result_summary: Saturated 2x2x2 model: main effects for a (-0.05), b (-0.07), c (-0.09) all small and only c marginal (p=0.05). All three 2-way interactions small and non-significant (ab p=0.09, ac p=0.46, bc p=0.50). The 3-way coefficient abc = +2.13, z=20.4, p≈0. LRT for adding the 3-way term: chi2=425.7 (df=1), p≈0. The interaction is purely 3-way.

analyses.code: df['triple_pos']=...; sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[['triple_pos']+all_features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable model with triple_pos plus all 35 features (continuous standardized). triple_pos coefficient = +2.30 (OR=10.0), z=40.6, p≈0. After conditioning on triple_pos, the main effects of feature_001, feature_021 vanish (p>0.5) and feature_014 retains a small NEGATIVE residual (-0.069, p=0.016) — i.e., outside the responsive subgroup, feature_014 alone is slightly worse, not better. feature_004 (-0.38), feature_020 (-0.15), feature_024 (-0.10), feature_026 (-0.19), feature_009 (-0.21), feature_027 (-0.06), feature_022 (-0.05), feature_029 (+0.06), feature_007 (+0.03) remain significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=1 ∧ feature_021=1 ∧ feature_014=1), the very high baseline response rate (~63%) is heterogeneous: at least one binary feature distinguishes 'super-responders' from non-responders within this subgroup with a large effect.

analyses.code: for each binary feature c: pd.crosstab(df.loc[triple_pos==1, c], df.loc[triple_pos==1, 'objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features dramatically suppress response within the triple-positive subgroup ('spoilers'). feature_026=1: response collapses from 71.7% (n=2025) to 18.4% (n=376), diff -53.3pp, chi2 p=7.6e-86. feature_009=1: response collapses from 69.1% (n=2125) to 18.8% (n=276), diff -50.2pp, chi2 p=3.3e-59. No other binary feature is significant within triple_pos. So feature_026 and feature_009 — which had only small main effects in the whole cohort — act as near-complete spoilers in the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple_pos × feature_026 interaction is large and negative (i.e., feature_026 specifically blocks the triple-positive effect), with no main effect of feature_026 outside triple_pos.
The triple_pos × feature_009 interaction is similarly large and negative.

The refined subgroup defined by (feature_001=1 ∧ feature_021=1 ∧ feature_014=1 ∧ feature_026=0 ∧ feature_009=0) has substantially higher response rate (~78%) than every other 5-way combination of these five binaries (each ~16-19%).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ triple_pos + feature_026 + triple_pos*feature_026)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction triple_pos × feature_026: coef = -2.44 (z=-16.7, p≈0). Main effect of feature_026 outside triple_pos = +0.019 (p=0.59) — i.e., feature_026 has zero effect outside the responsive subgroup but reduces the log-odds within it by ~2.4 (OR ~0.09).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ triple_pos + feature_009 + triple_pos*feature_009)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction triple_pos × feature_009: coef = -2.26 (z=-13.6, p≈0). Main effect of feature_009 outside triple_pos = -0.005 (p=0.90) — same pattern as feature_026.

analyses.code: df['refined']=...; df.groupby('refined')['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count']); also full 32-cell joint table across (001,021,014,026,009)
analyses.result_summary: Refined subgroup (n=1790) has response 78.6% vs 16.5% in all 47,599 others. Risk difference +62.1pp; chi-square p≈0. The full 32-cell joint table shows that ONLY this single cell is elevated (every other cell sits between 8.6% and 19.6%). Within triple_pos, having either feature_026=1 OR feature_009=1 reduces response from 78.6% to ~18.5% (n=611).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_014 as the candidate treatment, a systematic interaction screen will identify feature_001 and feature_021 as the strongest positive effect modifiers and feature_026 and feature_009 as the strongest negative modifiers; ordinal feature_004 will show a small positive interaction (i.e., feature_014's effect is somewhat larger in higher feature_004 values on the log-odds scale).

analyses.code: for each candidate modifier m: Logit(y ~ feature_014 + m + feature_014*m)
analyses.result_summary: feature_014-by-feature interaction screen across all 35 features. Top positive interactions: feature_001 (coef=+1.06, p=6.8e-103), feature_021 (+1.06, p=5.5e-102). Top negative interactions: feature_026 (-0.60, p=1.1e-16), feature_009 (-0.47, p=6.8e-08). feature_004 interaction: +0.097 (p=0.008). feature_020 (+0.071, p=0.005). All other binary and continuous interactions p>0.02 and small (|coef|<0.2). No further large interaction terms detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_001 as the candidate treatment, the same biomarker pair (feature_014, feature_021) will appear as the strongest positive interactions and (feature_026, feature_009) as the strongest negative interactions, confirming the symmetric nature of the 3-way structure.
Treating feature_021 as the candidate treatment, the same dependency pattern will appear, again confirming symmetry.

analyses.code: for each modifier m: Logit(y ~ feature_001 + m + feature_001*m)
analyses.result_summary: feature_001-by-feature interaction screen. Top positive: feature_014 (+1.06, p=6.8e-103), feature_021 (+0.88, p=1.1e-78). Top negative: feature_026 (-0.38, p=3.3e-08), feature_009 (-0.28, p=5.0e-04). All other interactions small (|coef|<0.2) and at most marginally significant (feature_032 +0.19, p=0.029; feature_008 +0.14, p=0.037). Pattern matches the symmetric 3-way structure.

analyses.code: for each modifier m: Logit(y ~ feature_021 + m + feature_021*m)
analyses.result_summary: feature_021-by-feature interaction screen. Top positive: feature_014 (+1.05, p=5.5e-102), feature_001 (+0.88, p=1.1e-78). Top negative: feature_009 (-0.41, p=4.7e-07), feature_026 (-0.31, p=6.0e-06). feature_024 +0.068 (p=0.004), feature_020 +0.050 (p=0.039). All other interactions p>0.05. The structure is fully symmetric in (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A greedy recursive subgroup search (manual depth-5 tree splitting on Gini reduction) will independently rediscover the refined subgroup as the unique high-response leaf, confirming it is the dominant signal in the data rather than an artifact of pre-specified hypotheses.

analyses.code: manual greedy tree: best split at each node maximizes p(1-p)*n reduction; min_leaf=200, max_depth=5
analyses.result_summary: Top splits chosen by the greedy tree: root → feature_004<=0; left → feature_021<=0 vs >0; the right side (feature_021=1) → feature_014; then → feature_001; then → feature_026. The two leaves with the highest response are: (feature_004=0 ∧ feature_001=1 ∧ feature_021=1 ∧ feature_014=1 ∧ feature_026=0) → n=693, p=0.791; and (feature_004>0 ∧ feature_001=1 ∧ feature_021=1 ∧ feature_014=1 ∧ feature_026=0) → n=1332, p=0.678. Both are subsets of the refined subgroup, exactly as expected, and feature_004 is the dominant within-subgroup modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 act as spoilers ONLY in the triple-positive subgroup; in the 'two-of-three' subgroup (exactly two of feature_001, feature_021, feature_014 positive) they have no effect on response.

analyses.code: df['two_of_3'] = (df[['feature_001','feature_021','feature_014']].sum(1)==2).astype(int); df.loc[two_of_3==1].groupby(['feature_026','feature_009'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within two-of-three (n=12,821): response rates by (feature_026, feature_009) are 16.2% (00, n=9818), 16.8% (01, n=1089), 16.4% (10, n=1692), 18.5% (11, n=222). Differences are small and non-significant. Confirms that the spoiler effect is specific to the triple-positive interaction rather than a general suppressor of any of the three components.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the refined subgroup (n=1790, response 78.6%), no additional binary feature has a significant residual modifier effect; the responsiveness is essentially uniform across other binaries.
The ordinal feature_004 strongly modifies response within the refined subgroup, with monotone decreasing rates from feature_004=0 → 1 → 2.
Among continuous features, feature_020 is the only feature that modifies response within the refined subgroup, with higher feature_020 associated with lower response.

analyses.code: for each binary feature: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(refined_sub[c], refined_sub['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Within the refined subgroup, no binary feature reaches p<0.05. Smallest p-values: feature_015 (p=0.12), feature_019 (p=0.13), feature_032 (p=0.12), feature_016 (p=0.15) — all small absolute differences (5-6pp). The refined responsiveness is robust to all other binary covariates.

analyses.code: df.loc[refined==1].groupby('feature_004')['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count'])
analyses.result_summary: Within refined: feature_004=0 → 86.0% (n=617); feature_004=1 → 77.2% (n=902); feature_004=2 → 66.8% (n=271). Strong monotone decrease (~9-10pp per level). chi-square p≈3e-9.

analyses.code: for each continuous feature: pd.qcut quartile rates within refined; Logit(refined_sub[obj] ~ standardized continuous)
analyses.result_summary: Within refined: feature_020 quartiles 81.0/79.5/73.0% (Logit beta=-0.027, p=0.013) — modest negative gradient. All other continuous features (feature_011, 022, 024, 027, 028, 029, 031, 033, 034, 003, 002, 017, 023, 035, 007, 012, 013) show no significant modifier effect within refined (all p>0.07).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The refined subgroup × feature_004 interaction is small and only borderline significant on the log-odds scale (i.e., the refined effect attenuates only modestly with worse feature_004), and the refined effect is large and significant within every level of feature_004.
After full multivariable adjustment for all 35 features, the refined indicator has an extremely large adjusted odds ratio (>10) and remains the dominant predictor of objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ refined + feature_004 + refined*feature_004); also stratified by feature_004
analyses.result_summary: refined × feature_004 interaction: coef = -0.17, z=-1.96, p=0.05 (borderline). Stratified within feature_004=0: refined → 86.0% (n=617) vs 20.8% (n=16,975) — risk diff +65pp. Within feature_004=1: refined → 77.2% (n=902) vs 15.2% (n=23,754) — diff +62pp. Within feature_004=2: refined → 66.8% (n=271) vs 10.8% (n=7,083) — diff +56pp. The refined effect is clinically large at every level of feature_004.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[['refined']+all_features_standardized])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted multivariable model with refined indicator + all 35 features. refined coefficient = +3.06 (OR=21.4), z>40, p≈0. After adjustment, the refined effect remains overwhelmingly the largest in the model. Other significant covariates: feature_004 (-0.38, OR per unit=0.68), feature_020 (-0.15), feature_024 (-0.10), feature_026 (-0.19), feature_009 (-0.21), feature_027 (-0.06), feature_022 (-0.05), feature_029 (+0.06), feature_007 (+0.03).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL CANONICAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: among the three binary features that behave as a positive treatment-marker complex (feature_001, feature_021, feature_014), the simultaneous co-presence of all three (treatment indicator = feature_014; required co-positive biomarkers = feature_001 and feature_021) is associated with a substantially higher objective_response rate than otherwise — but ONLY when both spoiler markers feature_026 and feature_009 are absent. The complete subgroup definition is: {feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0}, with response rate 78.6% (n=1790) vs 16.5% in all others (n=48,210); risk difference +62.1pp; adjusted OR=21.4 (p≈0). Direction: positive (refined subgroup → higher objective_response).

Beyond the refined subgroup, the strongest non-treatment-related determinants of objective_response are: ordinal feature_004 (worse outcome with higher value, OR ≈ 0.68 per unit), continuous feature_020 (worse outcome with higher value), feature_024 (worse with higher value), and feature_022, feature_027 (small negative); feature_029 is a small positive prognostic factor.

analyses.code: df['refined'] = ((df['feature_001']==1)&(df['feature_021']==1)&(df['feature_014']==1)&(df['feature_026']==0)&(df['feature_009']==0)).astype(int); df.groupby('refined')['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count']); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: Refined subgroup: n=1,790, response 78.6%; complement: n=48,210, response 16.5%. Risk difference = +62.1 percentage points. Chi-square p≈0. Adjusted OR (in 36-feature multivariable logit) = 21.4 (z>40, p≈0). The full 32-cell joint table over (feature_001, feature_021, feature_014, feature_026, feature_009) confirms that ONLY this single cell is elevated; all 31 others have response rates between 8.6% and 19.6%.

analyses.code: see iter 12 final adjusted model coefficients
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds (full model, continuous features standardized): feature_004 -0.38 (z=-21.0), feature_020 -0.15 (z=-12.1), feature_024 -0.10 (z=-8.6), feature_029 +0.064 (z=+5.4), feature_026 -0.19 (z=-5.3), feature_009 -0.21 (z=-5.1), feature_027 -0.064 (z=-4.8), feature_022 -0.052 (z=-4.0), feature_007 +0.026 (z=+2.2). All other features non-significant after adjustment.
